scenarios.title: loading images page shows landscape image with correct src @positive @e2e
scenarios.userAction: Navigate to the loading-images demo page and wait up to 10 seconds for the landscape image element to load, verifying its src attribute contains 'landscape' (case-insensitive).
expectedAssertions: Landscape image element is attached to the DOM (auto-waited by toHaveAttribute)
Landscape image src attribute value matches /landscape/i within { timeout: 10_000 }

locatorTable.notes: An element's ARIA role is 'img'; its computed accessible name is its alt attribute. getByRole('img', { name: /landscape/i }) is tier-1 and equivalent to getByAltText(/landscape/i) for images. Strong circumstantial evidence: element ID and Java variable are both 'landscape', consistent with bonigarcia educational demos that use descriptive alt text. Unconfirmed from source code alone — reviewer must resolve Q3. Fallback if alt absent or mismatched: page.locator('#landscape') with WHY-comment 'Q3 unresolved — alt text not confirmed'.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q3 unresolved — alt text not confirmed
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Open https://bonigarcia.dev/selenium-webdriver-java/loading-images.html in DevTools and inspect . If alt attribute contains 'landscape', use getByRole('img', { name: /landscape/i }). If alt is absent or different, Stage 2 MUST use page.locator('#landscape') with the verbatim WHY-comment 'Q3 unresolved — alt text not confirmed'. Any silent promotion of the MED-confidence locator without reviewer DOM confirmation is a block-severity hallucination failure.
